is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum infections; dhfr and dhps genotyping of P. falciparum isolates.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports dhfr codons 51,59,108 and dhps codons 436,437,540,581,613 — all curated target positions (Table 1).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives numerator/denominator per codon, e.g. dhfr 59R 70/72 (97) Rukungiri, 48/51 (94) Kabale; dhps 437G 71/72 (99), 60/60 (100).

sub_country_location.evidence: Frequencies split by district/health facility: Bufundi (Kabale District) and Kebisoni (Rukungiri District), southwest Uganda — sub-national resolution.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Blood spots collected May–December 2005; single-year collection window bounds all frequencies to <=3y.

african_field_samples.evidence: Fingerprick blood spots from symptomatic patients at Ugandan health facilities in 2005; human field isolates in Africa.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical field study reporting new genotyping data (though it also includes a literature-review Figure 1/Appendix, the reported frequencies are original).

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples collected as part of the Highland Malaria Project (HIMAL) sentinel surveillance system ('Blood spots were collected from patients at these facilities in 2005 as part of the HIMAL'); potential overlap with HIMAL datasets.

supplement.evidence: Extractable per-locus frequencies and sub-national locations are fully present in Tables 1-3 in the main text; the online appendix/map concerns only the literature review, not this study's data.

assessment.summary: Primary field study of P. falciparum from Bufundi (Kabale District) and Kebisoni (Rukungiri District), southwest Uganda, collected May–December 2005. Reports extractable per-codon frequencies for dhfr (51,59,108,164) and dhps (436,437,540,581,613) by district (Tables 1-2). Sub-national resolution and single-year collection window satisfy all criteria. HIMAL surveillance origin gives medium duplicate risk.
